summary: Add the scoring engine with docs-aligned calculateOpportunityScore(), score breakdown/explanation output, high-risk penalty behavior, and focused tests.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable scoring module exists at lib/scoring.ts and exports calculateOpportunityScore() plus typed input/output contracts for score hints, score breakdown, score explanation, and final total score.

The scoring formula follows docs/07_data_model_and_scoring.md weights: intent 0.18, monetization 0.16, SERP weakness 0.18, toolability 0.18, user fit 0.14, build speed 0.10, minus risk penalty 0.06, with total_score rounded and clamped to 0-100.

Inputs from Opportunity Analysis scoreHints are normalized defensively: missing or invalid sub-scores use deterministic defaults, numeric values are clamped to 0-100, and string-like numeric values are handled without throwing.

High-risk opportunities are penalized strongly enough that an otherwise strong high-risk opportunity is ranked below a comparable low-risk opportunity and is not likely to enter a default Top 5 when safer alternatives exist.
The output includes score_breakdown-compatible numeric fields and score_explanation-compatible concise explanations for every scoring dimension and risk penalty.

Focused automated tests cover the formula, 0-100 clamping, high-risk penalty behavior, higher scores for toolable/weak-SERP/user-fit opportunities, missing/invalid hints, deterministic output, and score explanation shape.
README documents the scoring engine slice, score dimensions, formula behavior, risk penalty behavior, and the test command.
No scan orchestrator, database persistence, API route, UI, ranking page, provider setup, real network call, cron, auth, or paid service integration is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/09_codex_implementation_tasks.md Task 10, docs/11_development_plan.md M4, and docs/07_data_model_and_scoring.md score formula.
This is the narrow scoring-engine slice only. Persisting score_breakdown belongs to the later scan orchestrator/API task; this task only returns a persistence-ready shape.
systems_architect is required because lib/scoring.ts becomes a shared boundary for later scan orchestration and ranking.
domain_designer is required because scoring weights, risk penalty behavior, and explanations are product/domain semantics.
non_goals: No services/scan-orchestrator.ts, /api/scans/run, SearchRun state machine, Prisma writes, or Opportunity persistence.
No changes to agents, SERP provider, LLM client, database schema, seed data, app routes, components, or UI.
No real LLM provider configuration, environment-variable secret loading, network calls, paid service setup, auth, cron, deployment, or dashboard work.
No learned/model-based ranking, user feedback calibration, configurable weights UI, or Top 5 filtering implementation beyond deterministic scoring behavior tests.
